app.title: Jem Pet Scanner
app.tagline: Scan a pet-food barcode to see what's inside

scanner.pointAtBarcode: Point your camera at the barcode
scanner.distanceHint: Hold the barcode flat, about 6–10 in (15–25 cm) away, in good light
scanner.permissionDeniedBody: This scanner needs permission to use your camera. Please allow camera access in your browser settings and try again.
scanner.noCameraBody: We couldn't find a camera on this device.
scanner.notSecureBody: Camera access only works over HTTPS. Please open this app using a secure (https://) link.
scanner.genericErrorBody: We couldn't start the scanner. Please try again.

analysis.illustrative: Illustrative — this brand hasn't been confirmed with a veterinary nutritionist yet. Treat it as a starting point, not a final word.
analysis.productNotFoundTitle: We don't have data for this product yet
analysis.productNotFoundBody: This barcode isn't in the open product database we check. As more people scan, we'll be able to recognize more products.
analysis.brandNotFoundBody: We found the product, but haven't evaluated its brand against our criteria yet.
analysis.speciesNotCovered: {{brand}} mainly makes food for other species — we don't have a {{species}}-specific evaluation for this line yet.
analysis.layer3Intro: Some things pet owners commonly believe, and what the science actually says — evidence first, belief second, never treated as equally reliable.

signals.completeBalanced: Complete & balanced for this life stage

capture.title: Send us the label
capture.body: Take a clear photo of the label (ingredients side is most useful). We'll use it to add this product to our database.
capture.notePlaceholder: e.g. brand name, pet type, where you bought it
capture.pilotNote: Pilot note: during this test the photo stays on your device — it isn't uploaded yet. We're validating the flow first, then we'll wire up real submissions.

profile.step1Subtitle: Let's set up their profile so the app can tailor what it shows you.
profile.illustrationNote: These illustrations are temporary — our creative team will replace them with the full kawaii avatar set soon.
profile.lookSubtitle: Just the basics for now — full custom avatars are coming soon.
profile.shapeSubtitle: A prototype set for now — more breed-like shapes and coats are coming.
profile.step4Summary_puppy: We'll tune the analysis for a growing {{species}}.
profile.step4Summary_adult: We'll tune the analysis for an adult {{species}}.
profile.step4Summary_senior: We'll tune the analysis for a senior {{species}}.

dashboard.prompt: Ready to check what's in {{name}}'s food?

care.title: {{name}}'s care log
care.intro: Keep track of dates so you don't have to remember them. This is just a personal log — no reminders or predictions yet.
care.disclaimer: This log doesn't replace your vet's recommended schedule — check with them for what's due when.

search.noResults: No brands matched — try a different spelling.

editPet.title: Edit pet
editPet.deleteConfirm: Remove {{name}}'s profile? This can't be undone.

methodology.title: How we analyze
methodology.philosophyBody: We don't give pet food a good/bad score or a single number. A brand or ingredient list doesn't tell the whole story, and a single score hides the nuance that actually matters. Instead we show you verifiable facts and what veterinary evidence says about them — you decide with that information, not a verdict from us.
methodology.layer1Body: A handful of yes/partial/no facts, meant to be read in a few seconds: whether the brand meets veterinary-aligned (WSAVA) criteria, whether it's backed by real feeding trials, whether the product contains artificial dyes, and whether there have been recent recalls. These are facts, not a moral judgment — a gray badge just means we don't have that data yet, not that something is wrong.
methodology.layer2Body: A plain-language paragraph explaining what a brand's veterinary credentials mean in practice, based on our brand evaluation table (see below). We always cite what it's based on.
methodology.layer3Body: Some ingredients (by-products, grains) have a bad reputation that veterinary nutritionists don't support. We show the common belief and the evidence side by side, with evidence always given more weight — we never treat a popular belief as equally reliable as the science.
methodology.dataSourcesBody: Product identification comes from Open Pet Food Facts, a free, community-maintained database — its coverage is uneven, especially outside Europe, so many products (particularly Latin American brands) won't be found yet. Brand-level evaluations (Layers 1–2) come from our own curated table, built from public sources and the WSAVA global nutrition guidelines.
methodology.verificationBody: Every brand currently shown is marked "illustrative": none has been confirmed yet through the official WSAVA questionnaire or reviewed by a veterinary nutritionist. Treat today's evaluations as a starting point we're actively working to validate, not a final word.
methodology.disclaimerBody: This app provides general information to help you understand pet food labels and industry standards. It isn't a substitute for advice from your veterinarian, who knows your pet's specific health needs.
